name: Paris (France, with arms)

colors.symbolism: from the cope of Saint Martin of Tours
associated with the oriflamme of Saint Denis

description: A vertical bicolour of blue at the hoist and red at the fly, charged at the centre with the coat of arms of Paris: a silver ship on blue waves with a red chief sown with golden fleurs-de-lis.
history: An older version of the Paris flag, displaying the city's medieval coat of arms in addition to the blue-and-red civic colours. The blue-and-red colours have served as the civic emblem of Paris since at least the 14th century. This 'with arms' variant is still flown alongside the plain blue-and-red bicolour.

features.symbolism: the traditional civic colours of Paris (blue for Saint Martin, red for Saint Denis)
a silver ship on blue waves with a red chief sown with gold fleurs-de-lis; the motto reads 'Fluctuat nec mergitur' ('She is tossed by the waves but does not sink')
